content.question: Do short-term achievement gains for students selected into 6/8-year gimnázium programs translate into long-term labor-market earnings or career-quality benefits in adulthood, or do gains primarily reflect test-score inflation without persistent human-capital gains?
content.reduction_path: Follow longitudinal cohort data from Hungary tracking gimnázium versus non-gymnázium entrants from age 10 through age 30+, measuring earnings, employment stability, occupational skill requirements, and employer-assessed competencies, while controlling for selection characteristics and later vocational track placement.
